summary: Mandibular teeth experience higher incidences of tooth brush abrasion because they encounter greater pressure during chewing and have different holding habits affecting them asymmetrically; thus, these areas tend to show signs of abrasion more frequently. To address potential issues related specifically to the left side or other regions not mentioned here would require additional context beyond what was provided about general dental care practices and mechanical forces involved. Given only two options for answering "more commonly," we can conclude based solely upon prevalence data presented within our scope which aligns best with option A regarding lower jaw involvement over upper ones under typical usage scenarios described herein without needing further clarification outside those parameters set forth by available knowledge resources consulted directly through
